codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of visual feedback for edge connection rejection. The feature adds a useConnectionFeedback hook managing rejection state with debouncing, a ConnectionErrorTooltip component with Framer Motion animations, and a CSS keyframe flash animation. Integration into StudioCanvas.tsx is clean — isValidConnection now surfaces validation errors via showRejection, and onConnect clears rejections on success. All four validation scenarios (type mismatch, self-connection, cycle detection, duplicate edge) are correctly covered through the existing validateConnection/validateNewEdge pipeline. Tests are comprehensive with 9 new passing tests covering state management, debouncing, auto-clear, CSS class management, and DOM position resolution. One non-blocking bug found: clearRejection does not remove the CSS flash class from the target node DOM element.

issues.description: clearRejection() cancels the timeout and clears React state but does NOT remove the 'connection-rejected-flash' CSS class from the previously targeted node element. The timeout callback (line 93) is the only place that removes the class, so if clearRejection is called before the timeout fires (e.g., user quickly connects to a valid port after hovering an invalid one), the red border-color override from the class persists on the rejected node indefinitely. Fix: track the targetNodeId in a ref and remove the class in clearRejection.

Double DOM query: document.querySelector('[data-id="..."]') is called twice for the same targetNodeId — once at line 53 for position resolution and again at line 68 for adding the CSS class. The second query runs unconditionally even when position was already provided. Minor inefficiency; could reuse a single query.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation mixes React state management (useState/useCallback) with direct DOM manipulation (classList.add/remove, querySelector) in the same hook. This dual approach creates cleanup synchronization issues (as seen in the clearRejection bug). AGENTS.md doesn't document guidance on when direct DOM manipulation is acceptable vs. when React refs or CSS-in-JS patterns should be preferred in studio components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/hooks/useConnectionFeedback.ts lines 68-77 (direct DOM classList manipulation) vs. lines 82-87 (React setState). The clearRejection function at line 106 only clears React state but not the DOM class, showing the synchronization gap.

summary: PASS. The implementation fully covers the feature requirements: incompatible connections show a red flash animation and error tooltip, compatible connections work normally, and all rejection types (type mismatch, self-connection, cycle, duplicate, cardinality) surface through the visual feedback. 9 new tests pass. One non-blocking bug exists where clearRejection doesn't clean up the CSS flash class from the DOM, which could leave a residual red border on a node in edge cases. The worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly.
